Recommendations
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, as provided by COA Circular No. 2015-008 dated November 25, 2015, require the coordination and cooperation of the following personnel:
a.   Local Accountant-
Prepare the Journal Voucher to record the beginning balance of the road network and its components in the general ledger and the Local Road Networks Ledger Card (Annex D), respectively;
Support the JEV with the Registry of Public Infrastructures for Roads, working paper on the distribution of costs for the road components, and working paper on the determination of the depreciated replacement cost for road components with no available cost per registry;
Keep and maintain subsidiary records for roads and components for every road network;
Prepare a lapsing schedule for the computation of the depreciation for each component at the end of the year;
Disclose the total road network of the Municipality in the Notes to the Financial Statements of the Agency (Annex B).
b. Municipal Treasurer/ General Services Officer
Maintain a Local Road Inventory and Road Map;
Keep a complete Local Road Network Property Card for all roads and its components (Annex E).
Render a Report on Local Road Network of the Municipality (Annex A).
d. Municipal Engineer
Provide the local accountant and the general services officer with the complete description and cost segregation of road components for road projects.
e.  Inventory Committee
prepare the Report on the Physical Count of the Road Network System of the Municipality (Annex C), in accordance with this circular.
Management Comment:
The management replied with a letter dated April 6, 2020 and agreed to comply with the recommendation through the above specific instructions to the Local Accountant, Municipal Treasurer/ General Services Officer, Municipal Engineer and the Inventory Committee.

Direct the accountant to maintain special accounts in the General Fund, including the maintenance of complete subsidiary ledgers and preparation of the financial statements for these accounts to ensure adequate information as to the assets, liabilities and equity of each special account especially for monitoring purposes.
